title: Chapter 52
text: Chapter 45 Weeks Pregnant: 30 Animals on the Mountain: 10 Wyatt “I’ll get the rest,” I state to Calder as I squat and lift the three last boxes. “You guys go ahead.” My brothers nod and make their way down the steps and through the barn to carry the final load of Trista’s belonging to my place. “I’m getting a strange sense of déjà vu,” Trista says as she bends over to pick up a laundry basket filled with kitchen gadgets. I clear my throat loudly and she turns on her heel to eye me with that challenging twinkle. “You weren’t just about to pick that up, were you?” I narrow my eyes on her and set the boxes I’m holding down on the table. “Yes, I was,” she replies with a laugh. “It’s not that heavy.” “It’s also not necessary for you to pick up when I’m right here.” I stalk slowly towards the woman carrying my child, my eyes drinking in every growing inch of her with great satisfaction. She’s never looked more beautiful than she does right now, that I can’t get any luckier, but I’m always proven wrong the next day when she dares to look even more beautiful. “I shouldn’t have to remind you that you’re carrying precious cargo.” I cup her growing belly possessively, an act that sends a surge of pride through me every time. How I could have gone through our whole pregnancy without holding her like this...feeling the baby kick...watching her eyes light up every time she has a moment with the baby all on her own...it’s a sad, scary thought. And to watch her embrace the pregnancy more fully these past several weeks is a beautiful sight too. I respected her decision in the beginning to give it up and truly just be a surrogate. It’s a gift not many people can do. But I respect her even more for knowing her own mind and advocating for what she wanted. And if sharing custody was all she wanted out of me, I would have accepted that. It would have killed me because I think I’d been in love with this woman far longer than I realized, but I would have done what was right. But to sit here, holding her, loving her, and moving her into my place and planning a whole damn life with her. I don’t know what I did to deserve this. I half wonder if my dad is pulling strings up above because this is everything he ever wanted for me. Trista smirks defiantly up at me as she clutches my hands on her belly. “My therapist says my willful independence is called avoidant attachment disorder because of all my abandonment issues as a child. You want to unpack that further with me?” My lip’s part as my heart sinks in my chest. “Hey Lucky, I’m sorry I didn’t mean—” “Don’t be sorry.” She presses her hand to my lips to stop my yammering. “It helps for me to make light of what I’m learning about myself in therapy. It’s all a part of the work I’m putting in to not pass my fuckedupness down to our baby.” A sad smile tugs at the corner of my mouth. She started therapy almost immediately after the baby shower. She said she needed to work through this embracing motherhood stuff on her own and unpack years of childhood shit that I only know the highlights of. All I know is that if ever there is a day her parents try to walk back into her life, it will be one where I’ll need my own therapist to talk me out of beating the shit out of them. Trista says she’s scared she could be the same to our baby as her parents were to her, but anyone with eyes could see that if she loves our kid half as much as she loves all those critters down below us, this baby will live a full and happy life. And I’ll be there to help her through that. I pull Trista closer to me and press my lips to hers. “I love when you say ‘our baby.’” She smiles against my smile. “Well, she is ours and as much as I love you, I just want you to know you don’t need to comfort me through all my therapy stuff. I can handle it.” I glower down at her. “I will comfort my girlfriend anytime I damn well please.” She shakes with laughter, losing it every time I call her my girlfriend. I will admit it feels juvenile, especially with all we’ve gone through together, but she won’t let me put a ring on her finger yet, so it is what it is. “Fine, you can comfort me. Just know that it’ll take some time for me to get used to you doing stuff for me because I didn’t have that growing up. But I am working on it, I promise.” My eyes move from her lips to her eyes and back down to her lips. “You’re amazing.” “I know I am,” she replies, and I feel her inhale deeply as I kiss her forehead. “This baby will be even more amazing than me! Can you imagine the thought?” “She will be a force of nature just like her mama.” I move around Trista to hold her from behind and take in the barn apartment. This place has been through some crazy times, but nothing ever as significant as this moment right here. “And you’re sure we’re not moving things along too fast, right? Me moving in with you I mean?” Trista tips her head back to look up at me. “I really want this to work between us.” “We work.” I squeeze her tight to feel the certainly in my bones. “And it was either you move into my place, or I move into yours because I don’t like being away from you two at night.” I move my hands over her belly possessively. “And not for nothing, but my place smells better.” “I like the smell in here!” Trista argues proudly. “You would,” I murmur, and press my lips to her neck, inhaling her rosy scent. “And you’ll still get plenty of time up here when we turn this space into your office.” “My own office,” she says the words like they’re still a dream, a far off fantasy that she can’t quite visualize yet. But she has no idea what lengths I’ll go through to make her dreams a reality. Because this lucky woman right here has already made all my dreams come true.
